232	/-- **Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push–pull head.**
233	For composable scheme morphisms `f : A ⟶ B`, `p : B ⟶ Z` and `N : Z.Modules`, the
234	adjunction unit at `N` for `p` followed by the *head* of `pushPullMap` (the
235	pushforward of the unit for `f`, then the pushforward comparison) equals the unit
236	for the composite `f ≫ p` followed by the pushforward of the inverse pullback
237	comparison. This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
238	`η^{f≫p}` into the iterated units `η^p`, `η^f`; it is the reusable ingredient that
239	the functoriality (pentagon) law of `pushPullMap` repeatedly consumes when

540	`O_X`-module nerve `CechNerve`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp` consumer). -/
541	noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
542	  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
543	  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
544	  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
545	  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
546	
547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)

58	/-- The wide pullback over `S` of a family `(g k : Z k ⟶ S)`, viewed in `Over S`, is a limit fan of
59	the legs `Over.mk (g k)`: i.e. the wide fibre power over `S` is the product of the legs in the slice
60	`Over S`.  Project-local: Mathlib has `WidePullbackCone.isLimitOfFan` (wide pullback over a terminal
61	base = product of legs) but not this direct slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
62	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
63	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g] :
64	    IsLimit (Fan.mk (C := Over S) (f := fun k => Over.mk (g k))
65	      (Over.mk (WidePullback.base g))
66	      (fun k => Over.homMk (WidePullback.π g k) (WidePullback.π_arrow g k))) :=
67	  mkFanLimit _
68	    (fun s => Over.homMk
69	      (WidePullback.lift s.pt.hom (fun k => (s.proj k).left) (fun k => Over.w (s.proj k)))
70	      (WidePullback.lift_base _ _ _ _))
71	    (fun s k => by
72	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
73	      change (WidePullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ WidePullback.π g k) = (s.proj k).left
74	      exact WidePullback.lift_π _ _ _ _ _)
75	    (fun s m hm => by
76	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
77	      change m.left = WidePullback.lift _ _ _
78	      apply WidePullback.hom_ext
79	      · intro k
80	        rw [WidePullback.lift_π]
81	        exact congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm k)
82	      · rw [WidePullback.lift_base]
83	        exact Over.w m)

117	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
118	    simp [overSigmaDescCofan, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
119	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext

121	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)

137	/-- Splitting a finite product off its head: `∏ᶜ X ≅ X 0 ⨯ ∏ᶜ (fun i => X i.succ)` for
138	`X : Fin (n+1) → C`.  Built directly as a limit fan (`mkFanLimit`) — Mathlib has no `Fin`-succ
139	categorical product split.  This is the recursion that lets the wide fibre power
140	`∏ᶜ (Fin(p+2) copies)` be peeled into `head ×_S ∏ᶜ (Fin(p+1) copies)` in the slice during the
141	induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`.  Project-local. -/
142	noncomputable def prodFinSuccIso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ}
143	    (X : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
144	    (∏ᶜ X) ≅ X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) := by
145	  refine (productIsProduct X).conePointUniqueUpToIso (mkFanLimit
146	    (Fan.mk (X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ))
147	      (fun j => Fin.cases prod.fst (fun i => prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) j))
148	    (fun s => prod.lift (s.proj 0) (Pi.lift (fun i : Fin n => s.proj i.succ)))
149	    (fun s j => ?_) (fun s m hm => ?_))
150	  · refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ j
151	    · change prod.lift (s.proj 0) _ ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0
152	      rw [prod.lift_fst]
153	    · intro i
154	      change prod.lift _ _ ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ
155	      rw [← Category.assoc, prod.lift_snd, Pi.lift_π]
156	  · apply prod.hom_ext
157	    · rw [prod.lift_fst]
158	      have := hm 0; change m ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0 at this; exact this
159	    · rw [prod.lift_snd]
160	      apply Pi.hom_ext; intro i
161	      rw [Pi.lift_π]
162	      have := hm i.succ
163	      change m ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ at this
164	      rw [← Category.assoc] at this; exact this

316	  set pA := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A with hpA
317	  set pAB := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B) with hpAB
318	  have hA : (∐ A).hom = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq A
319	  have hAB : (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
320	      = pAB.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq (fun i => A i ⨯ B)
321	  clear_value pA pAB
322	  have hAB' : pAB.inv ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
323	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := by
324	    rw [hAB]; simp
325	  have hcond : (∐ A).hom ≫ 𝟙 S = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
326	    rw [Category.comp_id]; exact hA
327	  refine Over.isoMk (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B ≪≫
328	    asIso (pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom
329	      pA.hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) hcond (by simp)) ≪≫
330	    (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).symm ≪≫
331	    asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv)) ≪≫
332	    pAB.symm) ?_
333	  show _ ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom = ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
334	  have hR : ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
335	      = (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫ pullback.fst (∐ A).hom B.hom ≫ (∐ A).hom := by
336	    rw [← Over.w (prod.fst (X := ∐ A) (Y := B)), ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst, Category.assoc]
337	  have e3eq : (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).inv ≫
338	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom)
339	      = pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom ≫
340	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
341	    rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, cf_hom_fst]
342	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
343	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
344	  have e4eq : Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv) ≫
345	        Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom)
346	        = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom) := by
347	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
348	    rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
349	      Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, ← Over.w (prod.fst (X := A i) (Y := B)),
350	      ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_inv_fst_assoc]
351	  rw [hR]
352	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
353	  simp only [Iso.symm_hom]
354	  erw [hAB']
355	  rw [e4eq, e3eq]
356	  congr 1
357	  rw [← Category.assoc]
358	  simp only [pullback.map]
359	  rw [pullback.lift_fst, Category.assoc]
360	  exact congrArg _ hA.symm
361	
362	
363	/-- Right-handed one-sided distributivity in `Over S`: `A ⨯ (∐ᵢ Yᵢ) ≅ ∐ᵢ (A ⨯ Yᵢ)`. -/
364	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib_right {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
365	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
366	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : Over S) (Y : ι → Over S) :
367	    A ⨯ (∐ Y) ≅ ∐ fun i => A ⨯ Y i :=
368	  Limits.prod.braiding A (∐ Y) ≪≫ overProd_coproduct_distrib Y A ≪≫
369	    Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A)
370	
371	-- The inductive step chains six iso layers (`widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `prodFinSuccIso`,
372	-- two distributivity isos, the reindex), whose combined `whnf` over the nested fibre powers
373	-- exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
374	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
375	/-- Coproduct distributes over the `(p+1)`-fold wide fibre power of the cover map `∐ᵢ Zᵢ ⟶ S`,
376	in slice-product normal form (blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower`). -/
377	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
378	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C}
379	    (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
380	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
381	    (p : ℕ) → (Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
382	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (σ k)))))
383	  | 0 => widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero f
384	  | (p+1) => by
385	      refine widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
386	        prodFinSuccIso (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫ ?_
387	      refine Limits.prod.mapIso (overSigmaDescIso f).symm
388	        ((widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).symm ≪≫
389	          widePullback_coproduct_iso f p) ≪≫ ?_
390	      refine overProd_coproduct_distrib (fun i => Over.mk (f i))
391	        (∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k))) ≪≫ ?_
392	      refine Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => overProd_coproduct_distrib_right (Over.mk (f i))
393	        (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k)))) ≪≫ ?_
394	      have e7 := Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
395	        (prodFinSuccIso (fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons i τ k)))).symm))
396	      exact e7 ≪≫ coproduct_fibrePower_reindex p (fun σ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k)))
397	
398	end FinitaryPreExtensive
399	
400	/-- A coproduct indexed by `Option α` splits off its `none` summand:
401	`∐ Z ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ a, Z (some a))`.  Project-local: the coproduct reassociation that drives the
402	`Option`-step of the finite-index induction in `pushPull_coprod_prod` (Mathlib has `sigmaSigmaIso`
403	for nested coproducts but no `Option`-split). -/
404	noncomputable def sigmaOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (Z : Option α → C)
405	    [HasCoproduct Z] [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => Z (some a))]
406	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (Z none) (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a))] :
407	    (∐ Z) ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a)) where
408	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc
409	    (fun o => Option.rec Limits.coprod.inl
410	      (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι (fun a : α => Z (some a)) a ≫ Limits.coprod.inr) o)
411	  inv := Limits.coprod.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Z none)
412	    (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι Z (some a)))
413	  hom_inv_id := by
414	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
415	    rintro (_ | a)
416	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc]
417	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
418	  inv_hom_id := by
419	    apply Limits.coprod.hom_ext
420	    · simp [Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
421	    · rw [Category.comp_id]
422	      apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
423	      intro a
424	      simp [Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
425	
426	end CategoryTheory

428	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
429	
430	open Scheme.Modules
431	
432	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
433	
434	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Stub 1 geometric backbone
435	
436	The degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p])` is the `(p+1)`-fold
437	fibre power of the cover map `q = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f` over `X`.  Identifying it with the coproduct
438	`∐_σ (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` needs two geometric ingredients that Mathlib provides only in binary
439	form:
440	
441	* `widePullback_openImm_inter` — the wide pullback over `X` of a *finite* family of open
442	  immersions is the intersection of their open ranges (built here from `IsOpenImmersion.lift`
443	  and the wide-pullback universal property);
444	* the wide/iterated coproduct-distributes-over-fibre-power step (extensivity), still open.
445	-/
446	
447	open TopologicalSpace in
448	/-- Pointwise membership in a *finite* intersection of opens.  Project-local because Mathlib's
449	`Opens` infimum is interior-based, so the pointwise `mem_iInf` characterization only holds for
450	finite families. -/
451	private lemma mem_iInf_opens_of_finite {Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace Y] {κ : Type}
452	    [Finite κ] (f : κ → Opens Y) (x : Y) :
453	    x ∈ (⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) ↔ ∀ i, x ∈ f i := by
454	  rw [← SetLike.mem_coe,
455	    show ((⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) : Set Y) = ⋂ i, (f i : Set Y) from ?_]
456	  · simp
457	  · apply le_antisymm
458	    · exact Set.subset_iInter fun i => SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le f i)
459	    · have hopen : IsOpen (⋂ i, (f i : Set Y)) := isOpen_iInter_of_finite fun i => (f i).2
460	      have hW : (⟨⋂ i, (f i : Set Y), hopen⟩ : Opens Y) ≤ ⨅ i, f i :=
461	        le_iInf fun i => by intro y hy; exact Set.mem_iInter.mp hy i
462	      exact hW
463	
464	/-- The wide pullback over `X` of a finite family of open immersions `g k : Z k ⟶ X` is the
465	intersection open `⨅ k, (g k).opensRange`.  Project-local: Mathlib has the binary
466	`isPullback_opens_inf` but not this wide/iterated form, needed to identify each summand of the
467	distributed Čech-nerve backbone with a `coverInterOpen`.  (With `g k := 𝒰.f (σ k)` the target
468	open is exactly `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ`.) -/
469	noncomputable def widePullback_openImm_inter {κ : Type} [Finite κ]
470	    {Z : κ → Scheme.{u}} (g : (k : κ) → Z k ⟶ X) [∀ k, IsOpenImmersion (g k)] :
471	    widePullback X Z g ≅ (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).toScheme where
472	  hom := by
473	    refine IsOpenImmersion.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (WidePullback.base g) ?_
474	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
475	    rintro x ⟨w, rfl⟩
476	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_iInf_opens_of_finite]
477	    intro k
478	    have hcomp : WidePullback.base g w = (g k) (WidePullback.π g k w) := by
479	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, WidePullback.π_arrow]
480	    rw [hcomp, ← SetLike.mem_coe, Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
481	    exact Set.mem_range_self _
482	  inv := WidePullback.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι
483	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift (g k) (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (by
484	      rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, ← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
485	      exact SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le _ k)))
486	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _)
487	  hom_inv_id := by
488	    apply WidePullback.hom_ext g
489	    · intro j
490	      rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
491	        ← cancel_mono (g j), Category.assoc, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac,
492	        IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.π_arrow]
493	    · rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac]
494	  inv_hom_id := by
495	    rw [← cancel_mono (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp,
496	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.lift_base]
497	
498	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` as the coproduct of the member arrows
499	`Over.mk (𝒰.f i)` in `Over X`.  Project-local: the coproduct cocone of the cover arrow. -/
500	noncomputable def coverArrowOverCofan (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
501	    Cofan (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) :=
502	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f))
503	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Sigma.ι 𝒰.X i) (by simp [Sigma.ι_desc]))
504	
505	/-- `coverArrowOverCofan` is a colimit: in `Over X` the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)`
506	is the coproduct of the member arrows.  Proved directly from the coproduct universal property in
507	`Scheme` (each leg is `Over.homMk (Sigma.ι …)`).  Project-local. -/
508	noncomputable def coverArrowOverIsColimit (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
509	    IsColimit (coverArrowOverCofan 𝒰) := by
510	  refine mkCofanColimit _
511	    (fun t => Over.homMk (Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left)) ?_)
512	    (fun t j => ?_) (fun t m hm => ?_)
513	  · change Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left) ≫ t.pt.hom = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f
514	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
515	    rw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Over.w]
516	    exact (Sigma.ι_desc 𝒰.f i).symm
517	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
518	    simp [coverArrowOverCofan, Sigma.ι_desc]
519	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
520	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
521	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)
522	    simp only [coverArrowOverCofan, Cofan.inj, Cofan.mk,
523	      Discrete.natTrans_app, Over.homMk_left, Sigma.ι_desc] at h ⊢
524	    exact h
525	
526	/-- In `Over X`, the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` is the coproduct of the member
527	arrows `Over.mk (𝒰.f i)`.  Project-local component of the Stub-1 distributivity step: the inner
528	`∐ᵢ Uᵢ` of the fibre power, transported into `Over X`. -/
529	noncomputable def coverArrowOverSigmaIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
530	    (∐ fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) ≅ Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) :=
531	  (coproductIsCoproduct _).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (coverArrowOverIsColimit 𝒰)
532	
533	/-- Transport of the `m`-fold wide fibre power (constant family) of a single map `q : A ⟶ X` along
534	an isomorphism `w : B ≅ A` of the apex with `w.hom ≫ q = q'`: in `Over X` the backbones
535	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q))` and `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q'))` agree.
536	Specialized to `Scheme` (the hom-universe must be pinned for the `rw`/`simp` on composites to fire).
537	Used in `cechBackbone_left_sigma` to transport the cover-arrow fibre power across the
538	universe-reduction reindexing `∐ 𝒰.X ≅ ∐ (𝒰.X ∘ e.symm)`. -/
539	noncomputable def widePullbackBaseCongr {A B : Scheme.{u}} (q : A ⟶ X) (q' : B ⟶ X)
540	    (w : B ≅ A) (hw : w.hom ≫ q = q') (m : ℕ) :
541	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q)) ≅
542	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q')) := by
543	  have hinv : w.inv ≫ q' = q := by rw [← hw, ← Category.assoc, w.inv_hom_id, Category.id_comp]
544	  refine Over.isoMk ?_ ?_
545	  · refine
546	      { hom := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)
547	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q) k ≫ w.inv)
548	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hinv]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)
549	        inv := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)
550	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q') k ≫ w.hom)
551	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hw]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)
552	        hom_inv_id := ?_
553	        inv_hom_id := ?_ }
554	    · apply WidePullback.hom_ext
555	      · intro k
556	        simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
557	          WidePullback.lift_π_assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
558	      · simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base]
559	    · apply WidePullback.hom_ext
560	      · intro k
561	        simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
562	          WidePullback.lift_π_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
563	      · simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base]
564	  · simp [WidePullback.lift_base]
565	
566	/-! ## Stub 1 — Geometric backbone identification -/
567	
568	/-- The degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone object, in `Over X`, is `Over.mk` of the wide-pullback
569	base map of the `(p+1)`-fold fibre power of the cover map `q = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`.  This is a pure
570	unfolding of `coverCechNerveOver = Over.lift …`, `coverCechNerve = augmentedCechNerve` and
571	`Arrow.cechNerve_obj`; all the identifications are definitional.  Project-local first step of
572	the Stub-1 geometric backbone identification. -/
573	noncomputable def cechBackbone_obj_widePullback (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (p : ℕ) :
574	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
575	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)) :=
576	  Iso.refl _
577	
578	/-- The slice-product of the cover legs over a multi-index `σ` is the intersection open
579	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ`, as objects of `Over X`: combine `widePullback_overX_eq_prod` (slice product =
580	wide fibre power) with `widePullback_openImm_inter` (wide fibre power of open immersions =
581	intersection open).  Project-local σ-component of the Stub-1 backbone decomposition. -/
582	noncomputable def coverInterProdIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
583	    (∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (𝒰.f (σ k))) ≅
584	    Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
585	  refine (widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))).symm ≪≫
586	    Over.isoMk (widePullback_openImm_inter (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))) ?_
587	  exact IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))
588	    (WidePullback.base (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))) _
589	
590	/- Planner strategy:
591	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
592	in `Over X`.
593	
594	Route:
595	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
596	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
597	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
598	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
599	
600	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
601	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
602	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
603	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
604	
605	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
606	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
607	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
608	
609	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the
610	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
611	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.
612	
613	Key Mathlib anchors:
614	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
615	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)
616	- `ColimitCocone` machinery for the coproduct in `Over X`
617	
618	Difficulty: MEDIUM — geometric bookkeeping, not sheaf theory. -/
619	noncomputable def cechBackbone_left_sigma (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
620	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
621	    ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
622	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
623	  -- Universe reduction: reindex the cover index `𝒰.I₀ : Type u` to `Fin n : Type 0`, since the
624	  -- extensivity primitive `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (in `widePullback_coproduct_iso`) is Type-0-only.
625	  set n := Nat.card 𝒰.I₀
626	  let e : 𝒰.I₀ ≃ Fin n := Finite.equivFin 𝒰.I₀
627	  let f' : (j : Fin n) → 𝒰.X (e.symm j) ⟶ X := fun j => 𝒰.f (e.symm j)
628	  -- reindexing iso on the cover coproduct, and its compatibility with the descent maps

978	private lemma isZero_modules_of_isEmpty {Z : Scheme} [IsEmpty ↥Z] (M : Z.Modules) :

1007	    refine (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_isZero (Scheme.Modules.pushforward _) ?_).isTerminal

97	
98	/-- The arrow `∐ᵢ Uᵢ ⟶ X` (`Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`) attached to an open cover `𝒰` of a
99	scheme `X`. Its augmented {\v C}ech nerve is the geometric backbone of the
100	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
101	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
102	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
103	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
104	
105	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
106	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
107	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
108	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
109	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
110	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
111	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
112	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
113	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve
114	
115	/-! ### Push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules` — object and morphism bricks
116	
117	The geometric backbone above is lifted to a cosimplicial `O_X`-module by the
118	*relative-direct-image functor on the over-category*
119	```

section SectionCechBridge

open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
  Concrete.productEquiv
    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))

/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
      = ConcreteCategory.hom
          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ

/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
    (h : Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
  rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
      ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
  change Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
  rw [hf, hg]
  exact h

/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
  classical
  induction s using Finset.induction with
  | empty => simp
  | insert a s ha ih =>
      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]

/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `σ` of the
cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
intersection `⨅ₗ U (σ (δ i l))` to the full intersection `⨅ₖ U (σ k)`.  Factored as a
named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` term. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
      ⟶ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) :=
  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op

/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
coordinates — exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
    (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) :
    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom
          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) σ
      = ∑ i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
  classical
  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
      show ConcreteCategory.hom
            (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ)
            (ConcreteCategory.hom
              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
          = ConcreteCategory.hom
              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
                ≫ Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) t
          from rfl,
      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]
  congr 1
  have hmap : (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).map (SimplexCategory.δ i) ≫ Pi.π _ σ
      = Pi.π _ (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i :=
    Pi.lift_π _ σ
  rw [CosimplicialObject.δ, hmap]
  rfl

end SectionCechBridge

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)

The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting

/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N

instance : Category X.Modules where
  Hom := Modules.Hom
  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

section Functor

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

end Functor

variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}

section Presheaf

/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf

/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)

instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

variable (M) in
@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
  M.val.map_smul _ _ _

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  ext U x
  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M

@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
    eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]

lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)

@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
    Iso.hom_inv_id]
  simp

end Restriction

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

659	private lemma isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {P Q T : C}
660	    [HasBinaryProduct P Q] {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q}
661	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk α β)) : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift α β) := by
662	  have heq : (h.conePointUniqueUpToIso (prodIsProd P Q)).hom = Limits.prod.lift α β := by

664	    · have := h.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp (prodIsProd P Q) ⟨WalkingPair.left⟩

673	private lemma isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
674	    {P Q T : C} [HasBinaryProduct P Q] (G : C ⥤ D) [PreservesLimit (pair P Q) G]
675	    {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q} [HasBinaryProduct (G.obj P) (G.obj Q)]
676	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β))) :
677	    IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) := by
678	  have hcomp : G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q
679	      = Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β) := by
680	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
681	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_fst, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_fst,

683	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_snd, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_snd,

693	/-- The binary disjoint-cover decomposition comparison map of a module sheaf on a coproduct
694	scheme: the pair of restriction-to-component units. -/
695	private noncomputable def coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
696	    M ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
697	          (M.restrict (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)) ⨯
698	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
699	          (M.restrict (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)) :=
700	  prod.lift ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)

711	a module sheaf on `A ⨿ B` is the product of its restrictions-to-component pushforwards.  This is the

713	abelian sheaf), reflected back to `(A ⨿ B).Modules`.  Project-local leaf of the Stub-2 chain. -/
714	private theorem isIso_coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
715	    IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := by
716	  rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
717	  intro U
718	  set ι₀ : A ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inl with hι₀
719	  set ι₁ : B ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inr with hι₁
720	  set W₀ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₀ ''ᵁ ι₀ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₀
721	  set W₁ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₁ ''ᵁ ι₁ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₁
722	  have h₂ : W₀ ⊓ W₁ = ⊥ := by
723	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
724	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
725	    rw [← inf_inf_distrib_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).inf_eq_bot, bot_inf_eq]
726	  have h₁ : W₀ ⊔ W₁ = U := by
727	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
728	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
729	    rw [← inf_sup_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).sup_eq_top, top_inf_eq]
730	  -- the two restriction-unit legs at `U`, identified as presheaf restriction maps
731	  set u₀ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₀).unit.app M with hu₀
732	  set u₁ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₁).unit.app M with hu₁
733	  have hleg₀ : u₀.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
734	    rw [hu₀, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
735	  have hleg₁ : u₁.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
736	    rw [hu₁, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
737	  -- the disjoint-union limit on the underlying abelian sheaf of `M`, transported to `Γ(M, U)`
738	  have LimAb : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (u₀.app U) (u₁.app U)) := by
739	    have L := TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint
740	      (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab _) W₀ W₁ h₂
741	    refine L.ofIsoLimit (Cone.ext (M.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (congrArg Opposite.op h₁))) ?_)
742	    rintro ⟨(_ | _)⟩

744	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₀.app U

750	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₁.app U
751	      rw [hleg₁, show (homOfLE (le_sup_right : W₁ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
752	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op
753	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
754	      rfl
755	  -- reflect the limit up to `ModuleCat` through the evaluation functor
756	  set G := SheafOfModules.evaluation (R := (A ⨿ B).ringCatSheaf) (Opposite.op U) with hGdef
757	  have LimMod : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁)) := by

767	  exact Functor.map_isIso _ _

770	
771	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
772	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
773	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of
774	the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q` is, up to the canonical leg iso
775	`pushPullCoprodLegIso`, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit.  This lets the
776	canonical comparison `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` be matched against
777	the manifestly-iso chain through `coprodDecompMap`. -/
778	
779	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where
780	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
781	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
782	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
783	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
784	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
785	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
786	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
787	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
788	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso

790	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
791	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
792	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)

794	-- The final `congr 1` discharges the proof-irrelevant `eqToHom` over-triangle transports against
795	-- concrete pushforward/pullback objects, whose `whnf` exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
796	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
797	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
798	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
799	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
800	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
801	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
802	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
803	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
804	      (pushforward q).map
805	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
806	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
807	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
808	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
809	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
810	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
811	        (pushforward c).map
812	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
813	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
814	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
815	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
816	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
817	  subst wC
818	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
819	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
820	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
821	  rw [← hLAU]
822	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl
823	
824	end BinaryDecomp

826	/-- Push–pull on a binary coproduct of two legs is the binary product of the two leg push–pulls.
827	The forward map is the canonical `prod.lift` of the two push–pull maps of the coproduct inclusions
828	(the mandatory framing the downstream section-identification needs); it is shown to be an
829	isomorphism by matching it leg-by-leg, via the per-leg coherence
830	`pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), against the manifestly-invertible reference chain through the
831	binary disjoint-union decomposition `coprodDecompMap`.  Project-local L2 assembly
832	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`). -/
833	noncomputable def pushPull_binary_coprod_prod (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
834	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)) ≅
835	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
836	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
837	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
838	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
839	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
840	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
841	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
842	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
843	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
844	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the
845	  -- syntactic pin is essential so the `Category.assoc`/`prod.map_fst` rewrites below can match
846	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
847	  set idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inl).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inl))
848	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
849	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
850	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
851	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
852	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
853	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
854	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
855	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
856	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
857	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr
858	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inr).unit.app M)
859	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
860	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
861	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
862	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
863	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
864	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
865	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
866	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)
867	  -- (q_* snd)`), avoiding any `≫ prod.fst` projection that the surrounding pushforward objects
868	  -- make awkward to reassociate.
869	  have hcmp : Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr) = chainIso.hom := by
870	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
871	      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]
872	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
873	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
874	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)
875	          ≫ Limits.prod.map idiso₀.hom idiso₁.hom
876	    rw [Limits.prod.lift_map, Limits.prod.comp_lift, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc,
877	      ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, coprodDecompMap, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
878	    rfl
879	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by
880	    rw [hcmp]; infer_instance
881	  exact asIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr))

inverse := { obj Y := mk (hX.from Y), map f := homMk f }
  unitIso := NatIso.ofComponents fun Y ↦ isoMk (.refl _) (hX.hom_ext _ _)
  counitIso := NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ .refl _

/-- The induced functor to `Over X` from a functor `J ⥤ C` and natural maps `sᵢ : X ⟶ Dᵢ`.
For the converse direction see `CategoryTheory.WithTerminal.commaFromOver`. -/
@[simps]
protected def lift {J : Type*} [Category* J] (D : J ⥤ T) {X : T} (s : D ⟶ (Functor.const J).obj X) :
    J ⥤ Over X where
  obj j := mk (s.app j)
  map f := homMk (D.map f) (by simpa using s.naturality f)

/-- The induced cone on `Over X` on the lifted functor. -/
@[simps]
def liftCone {J : Type*} [Category* J] (D : J ⥤ T) {X : T} (s : D ⟶ (Functor.const J).obj X)
    (c : Cone D) (p : c.pt ⟶ X) (hp : ∀ j, c.π.app j ≫ s.app j = p) :
    Cone (Over.lift D s) where
  pt := mk p
  π.app j := homMk (c.π.app j)

/-- The lifted cone on `Over X` is a limit cone if the original cone was limiting
and `J` is nonempty. -/
def isLimitLiftCone {J : Type*} [Category* J] [Nonempty J]
    (D : J ⥤ T) {X : T} (s : D ⟶ (Functor.const J).obj X)
    (c : Cone D) (p : c.pt ⟶ X) (hp : ∀ j, c.π.app j ≫ s.app j = p)
    (hc : IsLimit c) :

content:
/-- Construct a morphism between categorical coproducts from a family of morphisms between the
factors. -/
def Sigma.map' {f : α → C} {g : β → C} [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g] (p : α → β)
    (q : ∀ (a : α), f a ⟶ g (p a)) : ∐ f ⟶ ∐ g :=
  Sigma.desc (fun a => q a ≫ Sigma.ι _ _)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma Sigma.ι_comp_map' {f : α → C} {g : β → C} [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g]
    (p : α → β) (q : ∀ (a : α), f a ⟶ g (p a)) (a : α) :
    Sigma.ι f a ≫ Sigma.map' p q = q a ≫ Sigma.ι g (p a) :=
  colimit.ι_desc _ _

lemma Sigma.map'_id_id {f : α → C} [HasCoproduct f] :
    Sigma.map' id (fun a => 𝟙 (f a)) = 𝟙 (∐ f) := by
  ext; simp

@[simp]
lemma Sigma.map'_id {f g : α → C} [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g] (p : ∀ b, f b ⟶ g b) :
    Sigma.map' id p = Sigma.map p :=
  rfl

lemma Sigma.map'_comp_map' {f : α → C} {g : β → C} {h : γ → C} [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g]
    [HasCoproduct h] (p : α → β) (p' : β → γ) (q : ∀ (a : α), f a ⟶ g (p a))
    (q' : ∀ (b : β), g b ⟶ h (p' b)) :
    Sigma.map' p q ≫ Sigma.map' p' q' = Sigma.map' (p' ∘ p) (fun a => q a ≫ q' (p a)) := by
  ext; simp

lemma Sigma.map'_comp_map {f : α → C} {g h : β → C} [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g]
    [HasCoproduct h] (p : α → β) (q : ∀ (a : α), f a ⟶ g (p a)) (q' : ∀ (b : β), g b ⟶ h b) :
    Sigma.map' p q ≫ Sigma.map q' = Sigma.map' p (fun a => q a ≫ q' (p a)) := by
@[simps]
def sigmaSigmaIso {ι : Type*} (f : ι → Type*) (g : (i : ι) → (f i) → C)
    [∀ i, HasCoproduct (g i)] [HasCoproduct fun i => ∐ g i] :
    (∐ fun i => ∐ g i) ≅ (∐ fun p : Σ i, f i => g p.1 p.2) where
  hom := Sigma.desc fun i => Sigma.desc fun x => Sigma.ι (fun p : Σ i, f i => g p.1 p.2) ⟨i, x⟩
  inv := Sigma.desc fun ⟨i, x⟩ => Sigma.ι (g i) x ≫ Sigma.ι (fun i => ∐ g i) i

section Comparison

variable {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] (G : C ⥤ D)
variable (f : β → C)

/-- The comparison morphism for the product of `f`. This is an iso iff `G` preserves the product
of `f`, see `PreservesProduct.ofIsoComparison`. -/
def piComparison [HasProduct f] [HasProduct fun b => G.obj (f b)] :
    G.obj (∏ᶜ f) ⟶ ∏ᶜ fun b => G.obj (f b) :=
  Pi.lift fun b => G.map (Pi.π f b)

@[reassoc (attr := simp), elementwise nosimp]
theorem piComparison_comp_π [HasProduct f] [HasProduct fun b => G.obj (f b)] (b : β) :
    piComparison G f ≫ Pi.π _ b = G.map (Pi.π f b) :=
  limit.lift_π _ (Discrete.mk b)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem map_lift_piComparison [HasProduct f] [HasProduct fun b => G.obj (f b)] (P : C)
    (g : ∀ j, P ⟶ f j) : G.map (Pi.lift g) ≫ piComparison G f = Pi.lift fun j => G.map (g j) := by
  ext j
  simp only [Category.assoc, piComparison_comp_π, ← G.map_comp,
    limit.lift_π, Fan.mk_pt, Fan.mk_π_app]


instance (priority := 100) hasProduct_unique [Nonempty β] [Subsingleton β] (f : β → C) :
    HasProduct f :=
  let ⟨_⟩ := nonempty_unique β; HasLimit.mk (limitConeOfUnique f)

/-- A product over an index type with exactly one term is just the object over that term. -/
@[simps!]
def productUniqueIso [Unique β] (f : β → C) : ∏ᶜ f ≅ f default :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit _) (limitConeOfUnique f).isLimit

/-- Any isomorphism is the projection from a single object product. -/
def Fan.isLimitMkOfUnique {X Y : C} (e : X ≅ Y) (J : Type*) [Unique J] :
    IsLimit (Fan.mk X fun _ : J ↦ e.hom) := by
  refine mkFanLimit _ (fun s ↦ s.proj default ≫ e.inv) (fun s j ↦ ?_) fun s m hm ↦ ?_
  · obtain rfl : j = default := Subsingleton.elim _ _
    simp

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The images of each component in the coproduct is disjoint. -/
lemma disjoint_opensRange_sigmaι [Small.{u} σ] (i j : σ) (h : i ≠ j) :
    Disjoint (Sigma.ι g i).opensRange (Sigma.ι g j).opensRange := by
  intro U hU hU' x hx
  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hU hx
  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hU' hx
  obtain ⟨rfl⟩ := (sigmaι_eq_iff _ _ _ _ _).mp hy
  cases h rfl

variable {g} in
lemma isEmpty_of_commSq_sigmaι_of_ne [Small.{u} σ] {i j : σ} {Z : Scheme.{u}} {a : Z ⟶ g i}
    {b : Z ⟶ g j} (h : CommSq a b (Sigma.ι g i) (Sigma.ι g j)) (hij : i ≠ j) :
    IsEmpty Z := by
  refine ⟨fun z ↦ ?_⟩
  fapply eq_bot_iff.mp <| disjoint_iff.mp <| disjoint_opensRange_sigmaι g i j hij
  · exact (a ≫ Sigma.ι g i).base z
  · exact ⟨⟨a.base z, rfl⟩, ⟨b.base z, by rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, h.w]⟩⟩

lemma isEmpty_pullback_sigmaι_of_ne [Small.{u} σ] {i j : σ} (hij : i ≠ j) :
    IsEmpty ↑(pullback (Sigma.ι g i) (Sigma.ι g j)) :=
  isEmpty_of_commSq_sigmaι_of_ne ⟨pullback.condition⟩ hij

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance [Small.{u} σ] : CoproductsOfShapeDisjoint Scheme.{u} σ where
  coproductDisjoint g := by
    refine .of_hasCoproduct (fun _ ↦ pullback.cone _ _) (fun _ ↦ pullback.isLimit _ _) ?_
    intro i j hij
    apply Nonempty.some
    rw [isInitial_iff_isEmpty]
    exact isEmpty_pullback_sigmaι_of_ne _ hij

instance : HasFiniteCoproducts Scheme.{u} where
  out := inferInstance

instance : MonoCoprod Scheme.{u} :=
  .mk' fun X Y ↦ ⟨.mk coprod.inl coprod.inr, coprodIsCoprod X Y, inferInstanceAs <| Mono coprod.inl⟩

/-- The cover of `∐ X` by the `Xᵢ`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def sigmaOpenCover [Small.{u} σ] : (∐ g).OpenCover :=
  (Scheme.IsLocallyDirected.openCover (Discrete.functor g)).copy σ g (Sigma.ι _)
  (discreteEquiv.symm) (fun _ ↦ Iso.refl _) (fun _ ↦ rfl)

/-- The underlying topological space of the coproduct is homeomorphic to the disjoint union. -/
noncomputable
def sigmaMk : (Σ i, f i) ≃ₜ (∐ f :) :=
  TopCat.homeoOfIso ((colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, TopCat.sigmaCofanIsColimit _⟩).symm ≪≫
    (PreservesCoproduct.iso Scheme.forgetToTop f).symm)

@[simp]
lemma sigmaMk_mk (i) (x : f i) :
    sigmaMk f (.mk i x) = Sigma.ι f i x := by
  change ((TopCat.sigmaCofan (fun x ↦ (f x).toTopCat)).inj i ≫
    (colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, TopCat.sigmaCofanIsColimit _⟩).inv ≫ _) x =
      Scheme.forgetToTop.map (Sigma.ι f i) x
  congr 2
  refine (colimit.isoColimitCocone_ι_inv_assoc ⟨_, TopCat.sigmaCofanIsColimit _⟩ _ _).trans ?_
  exact ι_comp_sigmaComparison Scheme.forgetToTop _ _

content:
/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**

For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
complex.

Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
    simp
  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
    congr 1

/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).

The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.

Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)

end AlgebraicGeometry
