1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationBase
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sub-brick A — Leg: the `coreIso_comm` chain
10	
11	The `coreIso_comm` chain (`abHom_finsetSum_apply`, `coreIso_comm_leg`, `coreIso_comm_coface`,
12	`coreIso_comm_sum`, `coreIso_comm`) expressing naturality of the degreewise section iso
13	through the Čech differentials. Depends on `CechSectionIdentificationBase`.
14	
15	Carries the residual sorry `coreIso_comm_leg`.
16	-/
17	
18	universe u
19	
20	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
21	
22	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
23	
24	open Scheme.Modules
25	
26	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
27	
28	/-! ### The `coreIso_comm` chain (`lem:coreIso_comm_leg` → `lem:coreIso_comm_coface` →
29	`lem:coreIso_comm_sum` → `lem:coreIso_comm`), built bottom-up per the iter-072 effort-break. -/
30	
31	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum.  (Local copy of
32	the `CechAcyclic` private helper `ab_hom_finsetSum_apply`.) -/
33	private lemma abHom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
34	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
35	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
36	  classical
37	  induction s using Finset.induction with
38	  | empty => simp
39	  | insert a s ha ih =>
40	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
41	
42	/-! ### Geometric seam for `coreIso_comm_leg`
43	
44	The per-leg coface naturality is proved by unwinding `coreIso_objIso` to the push–pull
45	map of the **backbone inclusion** `backboneIncl 𝒰 p τ : Over.mk j_τ ⟶ Y_p` (the morphism
46	produced by `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`), and then computing geometrically:
47	
48	1. the backbone inclusion followed by the `l`-th wide-pullback projection is the
49	   canonical component map `interProj` (`backboneIncl_proj` — the Stub-1 unwinding);
50	2. hence the nerve coface `δ^nerve_k` restricts on the `σ'`-summand to the open
51	   inclusion `interLegHom : U_{σ'} ⊆ U_{σ'∘δᵏ}` (`backboneIncl_nerveδ`, by `hom_ext`
52	   through the projections);
53	3. the evaluated push–pull of that open inclusion acts on the identified leg sections
54	   as the plain `F`-restriction (`pushPull_interLegHom_sections`). -/
55	
56	/-- The `τ`-summand inclusion `Over.mk j_τ ⟶ Y_p` of the degree-`p` Čech backbone:
57	the coproduct inclusion composed with the two backbone identifications read backwards.
58	By `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`, its push–pull map is the `τ`-projection of
59	`pushPull_sigma_iso`. -/
60	noncomputable def backboneIncl (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ)
61	    (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
62	    Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) ⟶
63	      (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) :=
64	  coprodOverIncl (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
65	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) τ ≫
66	    (overSigmaDescIso (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
67	      (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).hom)).inv ≫
68	    (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p).inv
69	
70	/-- `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`, rephrased through `backboneIncl`. -/
71	lemma pushPull_sigma_iso_π_incl (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ)
72	    (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
73	    (pushPull_sigma_iso 𝒰 F p).hom ≫
74	        Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
75	          pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))) τ =
76	      pushPullMap F (backboneIncl 𝒰 p τ) :=
77	  pushPull_sigma_iso_π 𝒰 F p τ
78	
79	/-- The over-level `l`-th wide-pullback projection of the degree-`p` backbone, landing
80	in the cover-arrow object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)`. -/
81	noncomputable def backboneProj (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (p : ℕ) (l : Fin (p + 1)) :
82	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ⟶
83	      Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) :=
84	  Over.homMk (WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) l)
85	    (WidePullback.π_arrow _ l)
86	
87	/-- Morphisms into the degree-`p` backbone are determined by the `p + 1` over-level
88	wide-pullback projections: the backbone is the slice product of cover-arrow copies
89	(`widePullback_overX_isLimit`). -/
90	lemma backbone_hom_ext (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (p : ℕ) {A : Over X}
91	    {u v : A ⟶ (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))}
92	    (h : ∀ l : Fin (p + 1), u ≫ backboneProj 𝒰 p l = v ≫ backboneProj 𝒰 p l) : u = v := by
93	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
94	  apply WidePullback.hom_ext (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
95	  · intro l
96	    exact congrArg CommaMorphism.left (h l)
97	  · exact (Over.w u).trans (Over.w v).symm
98	
99	/-- The Čech-nerve simplicial face intertwines the backbone projections: the geometric
100	coface followed by the `l`-th projection is the `δᵏ l`-th projection. -/
101	lemma nerveδ_backboneProj (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (p : ℕ) (k : Fin (p + 2)) (l : Fin (p + 1)) :
102	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).map ((SimplexCategory.δ k).op) ≫ backboneProj 𝒰 p l =
103	      backboneProj 𝒰 (p + 1) ((SimplexCategory.δ k).toOrderHom l) := by
104	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
105	  exact WidePullback.lift_π (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) _ _ _ l
106	
107	-- The `rfl` unfolds the whiskered augmented-cosimplicial packaging of `CechNerve`, whose
108	-- kernel `whnf` exceeds the default budget.
109	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
110	/-- The evaluated Čech-nerve coface is the push–pull map of the geometric backbone
111	simplicial face (definitional unwinding of `CechNerve`). -/
112	lemma cechNerve_drop_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) {p : ℕ} (k : Fin (p + 2)) :
113	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ k =
114	      pushPullMap F ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).map ((SimplexCategory.δ k).op)) :=
115	  rfl
116	
117	/-- The canonical lift of the intersection-open inclusion `U_τ ↪ X` against the
118	`τ l`-th cover member (an open immersion). -/
119	noncomputable def coverInterToMember (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ}
120	    (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (l : Fin (p + 1)) :
121	    Scheme.Opens.toScheme (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ) ⟶ 𝒰.X (τ l) :=
122	  IsOpenImmersion.lift (𝒰.f (τ l)) (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) (by
123	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, ← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
124	    exact SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le (fun j => coverOpen 𝒰 (τ j)) l))
125	
126	/-- Factorization property of `coverInterToMember`. -/
127	lemma coverInterToMember_fac (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ} (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
128	    (l : Fin (p + 1)) :
129	    coverInterToMember 𝒰 τ l ≫ 𝒰.f (τ l) = Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ) :=
130	  IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _
131	
132	/-- The canonical `l`-th component of the `τ`-leg in the cover-arrow object: lift to the
133	`τ l`-th member, then include into the coproduct. -/
134	noncomputable def interProj (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ} (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
135	    (l : Fin (p + 1)) :
136	    Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) ⟶ Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) :=
137	  Over.homMk (coverInterToMember 𝒰 τ l ≫ Sigma.ι 𝒰.X (τ l)) (by
138	    show (coverInterToMember 𝒰 τ l ≫ Sigma.ι 𝒰.X (τ l)) ≫ Sigma.desc 𝒰.f =
139	      Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)
140	    rw [Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc]
141	    exact coverInterToMember_fac 𝒰 τ l)
142	
143	/-- Mono-target rigidity in the slice: two over-`X` morphisms into `Over.mk g` with `g`
144	a monomorphism agree.  (Used to absorb the reindexing isos of the Stub-1 chain.) -/
145	lemma over_hom_ext_mono {A : Over X} {B : Scheme.{u}} {g : B ⟶ X} [Mono g]
146	    (u v : A ⟶ Over.mk g) : u = v :=
147	  Over.OverMorphism.ext ((cancel_mono g).mp ((Over.w u).trans (Over.w v).symm))
148	
149	/-! ### The Stub-1 unwinding: projections of the `widePullback_coproduct_iso` inclusions.
150	
151	Abstract lemmas (any finitary pre-extensive category): the composite of a `σ`-summand
152	inclusion with the inverse of the distributivity iso `widePullback_coproduct_iso` and an
153	over-level wide-pullback projection is the canonical `l`-th factor projection followed by
154	the `σ l`-th descent inclusion.  This is the geometric content of `backboneIncl_proj`. -/
155	
156	section WPCIproj
157	
158	open CategoryTheory.FinitaryPreExtensive
159	
160	variable {C : Type*} [Category C]
161	
162	/-- Head projection of the inverse of `prodFinSuccIso`. -/
163	private lemma prodFinSuccIso_inv_π_zero [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ} (W : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
164	    (prodFinSuccIso W).inv ≫ Pi.π W 0 = Limits.prod.fst :=
165	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk (0 : Fin (n + 1)))
166	
167	/-- Tail projections of the inverse of `prodFinSuccIso`. -/
168	private lemma prodFinSuccIso_inv_π_succ [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ} (W : Fin (n + 1) → C)
169	    (i : Fin n) :
170	    (prodFinSuccIso W).inv ≫ Pi.π W i.succ =
171	      Limits.prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun j : Fin n => W j.succ) i :=
172	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk i.succ)
173	
174	/-- Head projection of `prodFinSuccIso`. -/
175	private lemma prodFinSuccIso_hom_fst [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ} (W : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
176	    (prodFinSuccIso W).hom ≫ Limits.prod.fst = Pi.π W 0 :=
177	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk (0 : Fin (n + 1)))
178	
179	/-- Tail projections of `prodFinSuccIso`. -/
180	private lemma prodFinSuccIso_hom_snd_π [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ} (W : Fin (n + 1) → C)
181	    (i : Fin n) :
182	    (prodFinSuccIso W).hom ≫ Limits.prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun j : Fin n => W j.succ) i =
183	      Pi.π W i.succ :=
184	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk i.succ)
185	
186	/-- ι-compatibility of a `Sigma.mapIso` inverse. -/
187	private lemma ι_sigmaMapIso_inv {β : Type*} {f g : β → C} [HasCoproductsOfShape β C]
188	    (w : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) (b : β) :
189	    Limits.Sigma.ι g b ≫ (Limits.Sigma.mapIso w).inv = (w b).inv ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι f b := by
190	  have h := ι_colimMap (F := Discrete.functor g) (G := Discrete.functor f)
191	    (Discrete.natIso (F := Discrete.functor f) (G := Discrete.functor g)
192	      (fun j => w j.as)).inv ⟨b⟩
193	  exact h

195	/-- ι-compatibility of the nested-coproduct flatten/reindex
196	`coproduct_fibrePower_reindex`. -/

198	    {p : ℕ} (F : (Fin (p + 2) → ι) → C) (i : ι) (t : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :

200	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F (Fin.cons i τ)) t ≫
201	        Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i : ι => ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F (Fin.cons i τ)) i := by
202	  simp only [coproduct_fibrePower_reindex, Iso.trans_inv, Sigma.whiskerEquiv,
203	    Limits.Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc, Iso.refl_hom, Category.comp_id, Category.assoc,
204	    sigmaSigmaIso_inv, Equiv.symm_trans_apply]
205	  refine Eq.trans (Limits.Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc _ _ _ _) ?_
206	  exact Eq.trans (Category.id_comp _)

209	/-- ι-compatibility of a `Sigma.whiskerEquiv` inverse (the reindex layers of the
210	Stub-1 chain). -/
211	private lemma ι_whiskerEquiv_inv {β γ : Type*} {f : β → C} {g : γ → C}
212	    [HasCoproduct f] [HasCoproduct g] (e : β ≃ γ) (w : ∀ b, g (e b) ≅ f b) (c : γ) :
213	    Limits.Sigma.ι g c ≫ (Sigma.whiskerEquiv e w).inv =
214	      (eqToHom (by rw [e.apply_symm_apply]) ≫ (w (e.symm c)).hom) ≫
215	        Limits.Sigma.ι f (e.symm c) :=
216	  Limits.Sigma.ι_comp_map' _ _ _

221	/-- `coprodFirst_distrib` is compatible with the second projection (counterpart of the
222	recorded `cf_hom_fst`). -/
223	private lemma cf_hom_snd {S : C} (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
224	    (coprodFirst_distrib B b g).hom ≫ pullback.snd (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b =
225	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.snd (g i) b) := by
226	  rw [coprodFirst_distrib]
227	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
228	  rw [pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd, pcd_hom_fst]
229	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
230	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
231	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst]

233	/-- ι-compatibility of `coprodFirst_distrib`: the `i`-th coproduct inclusion of the
234	distributed pullbacks corresponds to the canonical pullback comparison along `Sigma.ι`. -/
235	private lemma ι_cf_hom {S : C} (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S)
236	    (i : ι) :
237	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => pullback (g i) b) i ≫ (coprodFirst_distrib B b g).hom =
238	      pullback.map (g i) b (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i) (𝟙 B) (𝟙 S)
239	        (by rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]) (by simp) := by
240	  apply pullback.hom_ext
241	  · rw [Category.assoc, cf_hom_fst, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, pullback.lift_fst]
242	  · rw [Category.assoc, cf_hom_snd, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, pullback.lift_snd, Category.comp_id]

244	/-- The slice structure map of a coproduct, through the coproduct comparison of
245	`Over.forget` (local copy of the `CechSectionIdentificationBase` private helper). -/
246	private lemma overSigmaHomEq {S : C} (A : ι → Over S) :
247	    (∐ A).hom = (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom ≫
248	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
249	  haveI : HasColimit (Discrete.functor A ⋙ Over.forget S) :=
250	    hasColimit_of_iso (F := Discrete.functor (fun i => (A i).left))
251	      (Discrete.natIso (fun i => Iso.refl _))
252	  refine (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv_comp_eq.mp ?_
253	  rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
254	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
255	  rw [ι_comp_sigmaComparison_assoc]
256	  show (Limits.Sigma.ι A i).left ≫ (∐ A).hom = _
257	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
258	  exact Over.w (Limits.Sigma.ι A i)

260	/-- ι-compatibility of the slice distributivity `overProd_coproduct_distrib`: the `i`-th
261	inclusion of the distributed products corresponds to `Sigma.ι ⨯ 𝟙`. -/
262	private lemma ι_overProd_distrib_inv {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)]
263	    (A : ι → Over S) (B : Over S) (i : ι) :
264	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => A i ⨯ B) i ≫ (overProd_coproduct_distrib A B).inv =
265	      Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι A i) (𝟙 B) := by
266	  rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq]
267	  refine Eq.symm ?_
268	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
269	  -- step 1: the slice `prod.map` corresponds to the `pullback.map` of the `ι`-square.
270	  have h1 : (Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι A i) (𝟙 B)).left ≫
271	        (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom =
272	      (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).hom ≫
273	        pullback.map (A i).hom B.hom (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.ι A i).left
274	          (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) (by rw [Category.comp_id]; exact (Over.w _).symm) (by simp) := by
275	    apply pullback.hom_ext
276	    · rw [Category.assoc, Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst, Category.assoc,
277	        pullback.lift_fst, ← Over.comp_left, Limits.prod.map_fst, Over.comp_left,
278	        ← Category.assoc, Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst]
279	    · rw [Category.assoc, Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_snd, Category.assoc,
280	        pullback.lift_snd, Category.comp_id, ← Over.comp_left, Limits.prod.map_snd,
281	        Category.comp_id, Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_snd]
282	  -- step 2: the coproduct comparison of `Over.forget` on inclusions.
283	  have h0 : Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j).left) i ≫
284	        (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv = (Limits.Sigma.ι A i).left := by
285	    rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
286	    exact ι_comp_sigmaComparison (Over.forget S) A i
287	  have hpA : (Limits.Sigma.ι A i).left ≫
288	        (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom =
289	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j).left) i := by
290	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg
291	      (fun w => w ≫ (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom) h0.symm) ?_
292	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
293	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j).left) i ≫ w)
294	      ((PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv_hom_id)) ?_
295	    exact Category.comp_id _
296	  -- the two stacked pullback comparisons compose to the `Sigma.ι`-square
297	  have h2 : pullback.map (A i).hom B.hom (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.ι A i).left
298	        (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) (by rw [Category.comp_id]; exact (Over.w _).symm) (by simp) ≫
299	      pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc fun j => (A j).hom) B.hom
300	        (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S)
301	        (by rw [Category.comp_id]; exact overSigmaHomEq A) (by simp) =
302	      pullback.map (A i).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc fun j => (A j).hom) B.hom
303	        (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j).left) i) (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S)
304	        (by rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]) (by simp) := by
305	    apply pullback.hom_ext
306	    · simp only [Category.assoc, pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_fst_assoc]
307	      exact congrArg (fun w => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ w) hpA
308	    · simp only [Category.assoc, pullback.lift_snd, pullback.lift_snd_assoc,
309	        Category.comp_id]
310	  -- step 3: the fused comparison is the inclusion of the distributed pullback factor.
311	  have h3 : pullback.map (A i).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc fun j => (A j).hom) B.hom
312	        (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j).left) i) (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S)
313	        (by rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]) (by simp) ≫
314	      (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom fun j => (A j).hom).inv =
315	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => pullback (A j).hom B.hom) i := by
316	    rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq]
317	    exact (ι_cf_hom B.left B.hom (fun j => (A j).hom) i).symm
318	  -- step 4: collapse the coproduct comparison of `Over.forget` on the target.
319	  have h4 : Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j ⨯ B).left) i ≫
320	        (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B)).inv =
321	      (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => A i ⨯ B) i).left := by
322	    rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
323	    exact ι_comp_sigmaComparison (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B) i
324	  have c1 : (Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι A i) (𝟙 B)).left ≫
325	        ((overProd_coproduct_distrib A B).hom).left =
326	      (Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι A i) (𝟙 B)).left ≫
327	          (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫
328	          pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc fun j => (A j).hom) B.hom
329	            (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S)
330	            (by rw [Category.comp_id]; exact overSigmaHomEq A) (by simp) ≫
331	          (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom fun j => (A j).hom).inv ≫
332	          Limits.Sigma.map (fun j => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A j) B).inv) ≫
333	          (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) fun i => A i ⨯ B).inv := rfl
334	  have c2 : (Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι A i) (𝟙 B)).left ≫
335	          (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫
336	          pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc fun j => (A j).hom) B.hom
337	            (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S)
338	            (by rw [Category.comp_id]; exact overSigmaHomEq A) (by simp) ≫
339	          (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom fun j => (A j).hom).inv ≫
340	          Limits.Sigma.map (fun j => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A j) B).inv) ≫
341	          (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) fun i => A i ⨯ B).inv =
342	      (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).hom ≫
343	          Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => pullback (A j).hom B.hom) i ≫
344	          Limits.Sigma.map (fun j => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A j) B).inv) ≫
345	          (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) fun i => A i ⨯ B).inv := by
346	    rw [← Category.assoc, h1]
347	    simp only [Category.assoc]
348	    rw [reassoc_of% h2, reassoc_of% h3]
349	  have c3 : (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).hom ≫
350	          Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => pullback (A j).hom B.hom) i ≫
351	          Limits.Sigma.map (fun j => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A j) B).inv) ≫
352	          (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) fun i => A i ⨯ B).inv =
353	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun j => (A j ⨯ B).left) i ≫
354	          (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) fun i => A i ⨯ B).inv := by
355	    rw [reassoc_of% (Limits.Sigma.ι_map (fun j => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A j) B).inv) i),
356	      Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
357	  exact c1.trans (c2.trans (c3.trans h4))

359	/-- ι-compatibility of the right-handed slice distributivity. -/
360	private lemma ι_overProd_distrib_right_inv {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)]
361	    (A : Over S) (Y : ι → Over S) (i : ι) :
362	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => A ⨯ Y i) i ≫ (overProd_coproduct_distrib_right A Y).inv =
363	      Limits.prod.map (𝟙 A) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i) := by
364	  rw [overProd_coproduct_distrib_right]
365	  simp only [Iso.trans_inv, ← Category.assoc]
366	  rw [ι_sigmaMapIso_inv (fun i => Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A) i]
367	  simp only [Category.assoc]
368	  rw [reassoc_of% (ι_overProd_distrib_inv (C := C) (S := S) Y A i)]
369	  -- residual braiding algebra:
370	  -- `(br (Y i) A).inv ≫ prod.map ι 𝟙 ≫ (br A (∐Y)).inv = prod.map 𝟙 ι`
371	  refine (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mpr ?_
372	  refine (Iso.comp_inv_eq _).mpr ?_
373	  refine Eq.symm ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans ?_)
374	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => (Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A).hom ≫ w)
375	    (braid_natural (𝟙 A) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i))) ?_
376	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
377	    ((congrArg (fun w => w ≫ Limits.prod.map (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i) (𝟙 A))
378	      (Limits.prod.symmetry (Y i) A)).trans (Category.id_comp _))

380	variable {S : C} {Z : ι → C} [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C]

383	/-- The over-level `l`-th wide-pullback projection of the constant-`Sigma.desc` fibre
384	power. -/
385	private noncomputable def overWPproj (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) (l : Fin m) :
386	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ⟶
387	      Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f) :=
388	  Over.homMk (WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => Limits.Sigma.desc f) l)
389	    (WidePullback.π_arrow _ l)
390	
391	/-- The `i`-th descent inclusion. -/
392	private noncomputable def overDescIncl (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (i : ι) :
393	    Over.mk (f i) ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f) :=
394	  Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι Z i) (Limits.Sigma.ι_desc f i)
395	
396	/-- The descent iso `overSigmaDescIso` sends coproduct inclusions to `overDescIncl`. -/
397	private lemma ι_overSigmaDescIso_hom (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (i : ι) :
398	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => Over.mk (f i)) i ≫ (overSigmaDescIso f).hom =
399	      overDescIncl f i :=
400	  IsColimit.comp_coconePointUniqueUpToIso_hom (coproductIsCoproduct _)
401	    (overSigmaDescIsColimit f) (Discrete.mk i)
402	
403	/-- Inverse projections of the slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
404	private lemma wpEqProd_inv_overWPproj (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) (l : Fin m) :
405	    (widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin m => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).inv ≫
406	      overWPproj f m l = Pi.π (fun _ : Fin m => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) l :=
407	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk l)
408	
409	/-- Forward projections of the slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
410	private lemma wpEqProd_hom_π (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) (l : Fin m) :
411	    (widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin m => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).hom ≫
412	      Pi.π (fun _ : Fin m => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) l = overWPproj f m l :=
413	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ (Discrete.mk l)

415	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
416	-- The chain unfolds seven layers of distributivity isos; the `whnf` cost on the nested
417	-- fibre powers exceeds the default budget.
418	/-- **Inclusion/projection characterization of the wide-fibre-power distributivity**
419	(the abstract Stub-1 unwinding): the `σ`-summand inclusion composed with the inverse of
420	`widePullback_coproduct_iso` and the `l`-th over-level wide-pullback projection is the
421	`l`-th factor projection followed by the `σ l`-th descent inclusion. -/
422	private lemma ι_wpci_inv_overWPproj (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) (p : ℕ) :
423	    ∀ (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) (l : Fin (p + 1)),
424	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ' : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ' k))) σ ≫
425	        (widePullback_coproduct_iso f p).inv ≫ overWPproj f (p + 1) l =
426	      Pi.π (fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k))) l ≫ overDescIncl f (σ l) := by
427	  induction p with
428	  | zero =>
429	    intro σ l
430	    obtain rfl : l = 0 := Subsingleton.elim (α := Fin 1) l 0
431	    obtain ⟨i0, rfl⟩ : ∃ i0, σ = fun _ => i0 :=
432	      ⟨σ 0, funext fun j => congrArg σ (Subsingleton.elim (α := Fin 1) j 0)⟩
433	    simp only [widePullback_coproduct_iso, widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero, Iso.trans_inv,
434	      Iso.symm_inv, Category.assoc, Sigma.whiskerEquiv, Limits.Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc]
435	    rw [wpEqProd_inv_overWPproj]
436	    have hb := IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp
437	      (limit.isLimit (Discrete.functor (fun _ : Fin 1 => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f))))
438	      (limitConeOfUnique (fun _ : Fin 1 => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f))).isLimit
439	      (Discrete.mk (0 : Fin 1))
440	    refine Eq.trans (Category.id_comp _) ?_
441	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => (productUniqueIso fun k : Fin 1 =>
442	        Over.mk (f ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
443	          ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm.symm fun _ => i0) k))).hom ≫ w)
444	      ((reassoc_of% (ι_overSigmaDescIso_hom f
445	        ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm.symm fun _ => i0))) _)) ?_
446	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => (productUniqueIso fun k : Fin 1 =>
447	        Over.mk (f ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
448	          ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm.symm fun _ => i0) k))).hom ≫
449	      overDescIncl f ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm.symm fun _ => i0) ≫ w) hb) ?_
450	    exact Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => (productUniqueIso fun k : Fin 1 =>
451	        Over.mk (f ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
452	          ((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm.symm fun _ => i0) k))).hom ≫ w)
453	      (Category.comp_id _)) rfl
454	  | succ p ih =>
455	    intro σ l
456	    obtain ⟨i, t, rfl⟩ : ∃ i t, σ = Fin.cons i t :=
457	      ⟨σ 0, Fin.tail σ, (Fin.cons_self_tail σ).symm⟩
458	    -- unfold one layer of the recursion and reassociate
459	    simp only [widePullback_coproduct_iso, Iso.trans_inv, Category.assoc]
460	    -- peel the reindex layer
461	    rw [reassoc_of% (ι_fibrePower_reindex_inv
462	      (fun σ' : Fin (p + 2) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ' k))) i t)]
463	    -- peel the `e7` (prodFinSuccIso) relabelling layer
464	    have he7 := ι_sigmaMapIso_inv (β := ι)
465	      (f := fun j : ι => ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
466	        Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ 0)) ⨯
467	          ∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ k.succ)))
468	      (g := fun j : ι => ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
469	        ∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ k)))
470	      (fun j : ι => Limits.Sigma.mapIso
471	        (f := fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
472	          Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ 0)) ⨯
473	            ∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ k.succ)))
474	        (g := fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
475	          ∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ k)))
476	        (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
477	          (prodFinSuccIso (fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) j τ k)))).symm)) i
478	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun w => Limits.Sigma.ι
479	      (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 2) =>
480	        Over.mk (f (Fin.cons (α := fun _ => ι) i τ k))) t ≫ w)
481	      ((reassoc_of% he7) _)) ?_

624	/-- The face morphism between intersection-open legs: deleting the `k`-th index of `σ'`
625	enlarges the intersection, giving the open inclusion `U_{σ'} ⊆ U_{σ'∘δᵏ}`. -/
626	noncomputable def interLegHom (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ} (σ' : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀)
627	    (k : Fin (p + 2)) :
628	    Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ')) ⟶
629	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ k).toOrderHom))) :=
630	  Over.homMk (X.homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le (fun j => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ' j))

539	noncomputable def widePullbackBaseCongr {A B : Scheme.{u}} (q : A ⟶ X) (q' : B ⟶ X)
540	    (w : B ≅ A) (hw : w.hom ≫ q = q') (m : ℕ) :
541	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q)) ≅
542	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q')) := by

546	      { hom := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)

548	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hinv]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)

550	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q') k ≫ w.hom)
551	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hw]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)
552	        hom_inv_id := ?_
553	        inv_hom_id := ?_ }
554	    · apply WidePullback.hom_ext
555	      · intro k

569	base map of the `(p+1)`-fold fibre power of the cover map `q = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`.  This is a pure
570	unfolding of `coverCechNerveOver = Over.lift …`, `coverCechNerve = augmentedCechNerve` and
571	`Arrow.cechNerve_obj`; all the identifications are definitional.  Project-local first step of

575	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)) :=

579	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ`, as objects of `Over X`: combine `widePullback_overX_eq_prod` (slice product =
580	wide fibre power) with `widePullback_openImm_inter` (wide fibre power of open immersions =
581	intersection open).  Project-local σ-component of the Stub-1 backbone decomposition. -/
582	noncomputable def coverInterProdIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
583	    (∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (𝒰.f (σ k))) ≅
584	    Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
585	  refine (widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))).symm ≪≫
586	    Over.isoMk (widePullback_openImm_inter (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))) ?_
587	  exact IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))
588	    (WidePullback.base (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => 𝒰.f (σ k))) _

590	/- Planner strategy:
591	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
592	in `Over X`.
593	
594	Route:
595	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
596	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
597	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
598	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.

601	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
602	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
603	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
604	
605	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
606	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
607	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
608	
609	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the
610	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
611	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.

614	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
615	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)

619	noncomputable def cechBackbone_left_sigma (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
620	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
621	    ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
622	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
623	  -- Universe reduction: reindex the cover index `𝒰.I₀ : Type u` to `Fin n : Type 0`, since the
624	  -- extensivity primitive `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (in `widePullback_coproduct_iso`) is Type-0-only.
625	  set n := Nat.card 𝒰.I₀
626	  let e : 𝒰.I₀ ≃ Fin n := Finite.equivFin 𝒰.I₀
627	  let f' : (j : Fin n) → 𝒰.X (e.symm j) ⟶ X := fun j => 𝒰.f (e.symm j)
628	  -- reindexing iso on the cover coproduct, and its compatibility with the descent maps
629	  let wZ : (∐ fun j : Fin n => 𝒰.X (e.symm j)) ≅ ∐ 𝒰.X :=
630	    Sigma.whiskerEquiv e.symm (fun j => Iso.refl _)
631	  have hwZ : wZ.hom ≫ Sigma.desc 𝒰.f = Sigma.desc f' := by
632	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
633	    simp only [wZ, Sigma.whiskerEquiv, Iso.refl_inv, Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc, Category.id_comp,
634	      Sigma.ι_desc, f']
635	  -- reindex the σ-coproduct back from `Fin (p+1) → Fin n` to `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`
636	  let reIdx : (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → Fin n => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f' (σ k))) ≅
637	      ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (𝒰.f (τ k)) :=
638	    Sigma.whiskerEquiv (f := fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → Fin n => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f' (σ k)))
639	      (g := fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (𝒰.f (τ k)))
640	      (Equiv.arrowCongr (Equiv.refl (Fin (p + 1))) e.symm) (fun σ => Iso.refl _)
641	  refine cechBackbone_obj_widePullback 𝒰 p ≪≫
642	    widePullbackBaseCongr (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) (Sigma.desc f') wZ hwZ (p + 1) ≪≫
643	    FinitaryPreExtensive.widePullback_coproduct_iso f' p ≪≫ reIdx ≪≫
644	    Sigma.mapIso (fun σ => coverInterProdIso 𝒰 σ)

646	/-! ## Stub 2 — Push-pull over the Čech backbone is the product over intersection opens -/
647	
648	/-- A morphism in `X.Modules` is an isomorphism as soon as its image under the forgetful functor
649	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` to presheaves of abelian groups is one.  `toPresheaf` reflects
650	isomorphisms (it factors through fully faithful functors), so this is immediate.  Project-local L1
651	reflection wrapper for the Stub-2 disjoint-union chain (blueprint `lem:isIso_modules_of_toPresheaf`). -/
652	theorem isIso_modules_of_toPresheaf {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
653	    [IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)] : IsIso φ :=
654	  isIso_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)
655	
656	/-- If `BinaryFan.mk α β` is a limit, then the canonical comparison map `prod.lift α β` into the
657	chosen binary product is an isomorphism.  Project-local categorical helper for the disjoint-cover
658	decomposition. -/
659	private lemma isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {P Q T : C}
660	    [HasBinaryProduct P Q] {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q}
661	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk α β)) : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift α β) := by
662	  have heq : (h.conePointUniqueUpToIso (prodIsProd P Q)).hom = Limits.prod.lift α β := by
663	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
664	    · have := h.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp (prodIsProd P Q) ⟨WalkingPair.left⟩
665	      simpa [Limits.prod.lift_fst] using this
666	    · have := h.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp (prodIsProd P Q) ⟨WalkingPair.right⟩
667	      simpa [Limits.prod.lift_snd] using this
668	  rw [← heq]; infer_instance
669	
670	/-- If a functor `G` preserves the binary product `P ⨯ Q` and the mapped binary fan
671	`BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β)` is a limit, then `G.map (prod.lift α β)` is an isomorphism.
672	Project-local: combines `prodComparison` naturality with `isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit`. -/
673	private lemma isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
674	    {P Q T : C} [HasBinaryProduct P Q] (G : C ⥤ D) [PreservesLimit (pair P Q) G]
675	    {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q} [HasBinaryProduct (G.obj P) (G.obj Q)]
676	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β))) :
677	    IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) := by
678	  have hcomp : G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q
679	      = Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β) := by
680	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
681	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_fst, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_fst,
682	        Limits.prod.lift_fst]
683	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_snd, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_snd,
684	        Limits.prod.lift_snd]
685	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β)) := isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit h
686	  haveI : IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q) := hcomp ▸ this
687	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) (prodComparison G P Q)
688

780	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
781	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
782	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
783	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
784	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
785	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
786	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
787	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
788	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso
789	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≪≫
790	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
791	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
792	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)
793

797	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
798	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
799	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
800	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
801	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
802	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
803	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
804	      (pushforward q).map
805	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
806	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
807	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
808	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
809	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
810	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
811	        (pushforward c).map
812	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
813	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
814	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
815	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
816	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
817	  subst wC
818	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
819	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
820	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
821	  rw [← hLAU]
822	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl

824	end BinaryDecomp

826	/-- Push–pull on a binary coproduct of two legs is the binary product of the two leg push–pulls.

829	isomorphism by matching it leg-by-leg, via the per-leg coherence
830	`pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), against the manifestly-invertible reference chain through the

835	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
836	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
837	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
838	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
839	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
840	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
841	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
842	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
843	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
844	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the
845	  -- syntactic pin is essential so the `Category.assoc`/`prod.map_fst` rewrites below can match
846	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
847	  set idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inl).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inl))
848	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
849	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
850	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
851	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
852	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
853	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
854	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
855	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
856	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
857	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr

859	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
860	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
861	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
862	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
863	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
864	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
865	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
866	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)

869	  have hcmp : Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr) = chainIso.hom := by
870	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
871	      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]
872	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
873	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
874	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)
875	          ≫ Limits.prod.map idiso₀.hom idiso₁.hom
876	    rw [Limits.prod.lift_map, Limits.prod.comp_lift, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc,
877	      ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, coprodDecompMap, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
878	    rfl
879	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by
880	    rw [hcmp]; infer_instance
881	  exact asIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr))

893	/-! ### Finite-index induction: push–pull turns a finite coproduct into a finite product
894	
895	The binary `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` is upgraded to an arbitrary finite index `ι` by
896	`Finite.induction_empty_option`.  The chain runs through four leaves:
897	* `pushPullObjCongr` — transport a push–pull object along a slice iso;
898	* `overSigmaOptionIso` — slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split;
899	* `piOptionIso` — the dual `Option`-product split;
900	* `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` — the empty base case.
901	-/
902	
903	/-- Push–pull objects transport along a slice isomorphism `e : Y ≅ Y'` in `Over X`.  The
904	push–pull object is a contravariant functor of its slice argument, so the forward map is
905	`pushPullMap F e.inv` and the backward map `pushPullMap F e.hom`.  Project-local
906	(blueprint `lem:pushPullObjCongr`). -/
907	noncomputable def pushPullObjCongr (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :
908	    pushPullObj F Y ≅ pushPullObj F Y' where
909	  hom := pushPullMap F e.inv
910	  inv := pushPullMap F e.hom
911	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.hom_inv_id, pushPullMap_id]
912	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.inv_hom_id, pushPullMap_id]

914	/-- Slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split (`sigmaOptionIso`): for a family of slice
915	objects `legs : Option α → Over X`, the coproduct slice object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc (·.hom))`
916	is isomorphic to the binary-split slice object.  Built as `Over.isoMk` of the underlying
917	`sigmaOptionIso` together with the structure-map compatibility.  Project-local
918	(blueprint `lem:over_sigmaOptionIso`). -/
919	noncomputable def overSigmaOptionIso {α : Type*} (legs : Option α → Over X)
920	    [HasCoproduct (fun o => (legs o).left)]
921	    [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)]
922	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (legs none).left (∐ fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)] :
923	    Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun o => (legs o).hom)) ≅
924	      Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc (legs none).hom
925	        (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).hom))) :=
926	  Over.isoMk (sigmaOptionIso (fun o => (legs o).left)) (by
927	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun o => ?_)
928	    rcases o with _ | a
929	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom,
930	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
931	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom, Category.assoc,
932	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])

934	/-- The dual `Option`-product split: for `W : Option α → C` the total product splits off its
935	`none` factor.  Project-local (blueprint `lem:piOptionIso`). -/
936	noncomputable def piOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (W : Option α → C)
937	    [HasProduct W] [HasProduct (fun a : α => W (some a))]
938	    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] :
939	    (∏ᶜ W) ≅ W none ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a)) where
940	  hom := Limits.prod.lift (Limits.Pi.π W none)
941	    (Limits.Pi.lift (fun a => Limits.Pi.π W (some a)))
942	  inv := Limits.Pi.lift (fun o => Option.rec Limits.prod.fst
943	    (fun a => Limits.prod.snd ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun a : α => W (some a)) a) o)
944	  hom_inv_id := by
945	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
946	    rintro (_ | a)
947	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Category.id_comp]
948	    · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, ← Category.assoc,
949	        Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
950	  inv_hom_id := by
951	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
952	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
953	    · apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
954	      intro a
955	      simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]

957	/-- The coproduct inclusion of leg `i`, viewed as an over-morphism into the descent object

960	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)] (i : ι) :

972	  Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl legs i))

1368	  -- The evaluation-at-`V` functor `X.Modules ⥤ Ab` (forget to a presheaf of abelian groups,
1369	  -- then evaluate at `V`); it preserves the finite product.
1370	  by
1371	  let Ev := (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ
1372	    (Ab.{u})).obj (Opposite.op V)
1373	  let E : X.Modules ⥤ Ab.{u} := Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ Ev
1374	  haveI hT : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape
1375	      (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X) := inferInstance
1376	  haveI : Limits.HasLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) (Ab.{u}) :=
1377	    inferInstance
1378	  haveI hE2 : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) Ev :=
1379	    Limits.evaluation_preservesLimitsOfShape _
1380	  haveI : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) E :=
1381	    @Limits.comp_preservesLimitsOfShape _ _ _ _ (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) _ _ _
1382	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X) Ev hT hE2
1383	  exact E.mapIso (pushPull_sigma_iso 𝒰 F p) ≪≫
1384	    Limits.PreservesProduct.iso E
1385	      (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1386	        pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))) ≪≫
1387	    Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ => pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F σ V)
1388	
1389	/-! ## Stub 5 — Complex-level iso: evaluated augmented Čech section complex ≅ augmented concrete complex -/
1390	
1391	/-- The concrete (non-augmented) section Čech complex over `V` for the restricted cover.
1392	Used as the base for the augmented complex in `cechSection_complex_iso` and
1393	`cechSection_contractible`. -/
1394	noncomputable abbrev sectionCechComplexV (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1395	    (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
1396	  sectionCechComplex (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V)
1397	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F)
1398	
1399	/-! ### Structural helpers reducing the augmented complex iso to a non-augmented one. -/
1400	
1401	/-- Applying an additive functor `Φ` degreewise to a cochain complex commutes with
1402	augmenting: `Φ(C.augment f) ≅ (Φ C).augment (Φ f)`, with identity components.  This peels
1403	the augmentation node off the evaluated complex `D` so the remaining identification is between
1404	the *non-augmented* complexes. -/
1405	noncomputable def mapHC_augment_iso {V₁ V₂ : Type*} [Category V₁] [Category V₂]
1406	    [Preadditive V₁] [Preadditive V₂] (Φ : V₁ ⥤ V₂) (hΦ : Φ.Additive)
1407	    (C : CochainComplex V₁ ℕ) {Y : V₁} (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) :
1408	    (Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (C.augment f w) ≅
1409	      CochainComplex.augment ((Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj C) (Φ.map f)
1410	        (by
1411	          haveI := hΦ
1412	          change Φ.map f ≫ Φ.map (C.d 0 1) = 0
1413	          rw [← Functor.map_comp, w, Φ.map_zero]) := by
1414	  haveI := hΦ
1415	  refine HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
1416	    (fun i => match i with | 0 => Iso.refl _ | _ + 1 => Iso.refl _) ?_
1417	  intro i j hij
1418	  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
1419	  match i with
1420	  | 0 => simp [CochainComplex.augment]
1421	  | n + 1 => simp [CochainComplex.augment]
1422	
1423	/-- The augmentation condition `Φ(f) ≫ d⁰ = 0` survives applying an additive functor `Φ`
1424	degreewise, given the original condition `f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0`. -/
1425	lemma map_augment_cond {V₁ V₂ : Type*} [Category V₁] [Category V₂]
1426	    [Preadditive V₁] [Preadditive V₂] (Φ : V₁ ⥤ V₂) (hΦ : Φ.Additive)
1427	    (C : CochainComplex V₁ ℕ) {Y : V₁} (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) :
1428	    Φ.map f ≫ ((Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj C).d 0 1 = 0 := by
1429	  haveI := hΦ
1430	  change Φ.map f ≫ Φ.map (C.d 0 1) = 0
1431	  rw [← Functor.map_comp, w, Φ.map_zero]
1432	
1433	/-- An isomorphism of augmented cochain complexes assembled from an isomorphism `φ` of the
1434	base complexes, an isomorphism `eY` of the augmentation objects, and the compatibility square
1435	`f ≫ φ₀ = eY ≫ f'` of the augmentation maps. -/
1436	noncomputable def augmentCochainIso {V : Type*} [Category V] [Preadditive V]
1437	    {C C' : CochainComplex V ℕ} (φ : C ≅ C') {Y Y' : V} (eY : Y ≅ Y')
1438	    (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) (f' : Y' ⟶ C'.X 0) (w' : f' ≫ C'.d 0 1 = 0)
1439	    (hcompat : f ≫ (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp φ 0).hom = eY.hom ≫ f') :
1440	    CochainComplex.augment C f w ≅ CochainComplex.augment C' f' w' := by
1441	  refine HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
1442	    (fun i => match i with | 0 => eY | n + 1 => HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp φ n) ?_
1443	  intro i j hij
1444	  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
1445	  match i with
1446	  | 0 => exact hcompat.symm
1447	  | n + 1 =>
1448	    simp only [CochainComplex.augment_d_succ_succ, HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp]
1449	    exact φ.hom.comm n (n + 1)
1450	
1451	/-! ### The non-augmented core comparison `coreIso` (blueprint `lem:coreIso_*`).
1452	
1453	The three lemmas below build, degreewise object iso + differential match, the non-augmented
1454	core iso `(G_V ∘ Ψ) Č•(𝒰, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰', F)` that `cechSection_complex_iso` glues to the
1455	augmentation data via `augmentCochainIso`. -/
1456	
1457	/-- **Open-meet identity** (`lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib`): intersecting the cover-meet
1458	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ = ⨅ₖ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)` with an open `V` distributes factorwise over the
1459	nonempty `Fin (p+1)`-indexed meet. -/
1460	lemma coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ}
1461	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
1462	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V) := by
1463	  rw [coverInterOpen]
1464	  exact iInf_inf
1465	
1466	/-- **Degreewise object iso of the non-augmented core comparison** (`lem:coreIso_obj_iso`).
1467	The degree-`p` term of the evaluated non-augmented backbone complex
1468	`Γ(V, Ψ(pushPullObj F Y_p))` is isomorphic, as an abelian group, to the degree-`p` term
1469	`∏_σ Γ(⨅ₖ(coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V), F)` of the concrete restricted section Čech complex.
1470	This is `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) post-composed with the factorwise reindexing along
1471	`coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf`. -/
1472	noncomputable def coreIso_objIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1473	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
1474	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
1475	          (pushPullObj F
1476	            ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
1477	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
1478	      (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X p :=
1479	  pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F p V ≪≫
1480	    Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1481	      eqToIso (congrArg
1482	        (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))
1483	        (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 σ V)))
1484	
1485	
1486	end AlgebraicGeometry
1487
